title: iris reticulata subsp. reticulata
intro: Iris reticulata subsp. reticulata, commonly known as the netted iris, is a sub-species of the iris reticulata plant, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and net-like pattern on the leaves. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its flowers and roots containing iridin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of iris reticulata subsp. reticulata include its potential use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. The plant is typically prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
